qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe deserialization in RMI context propagation allows arbitrary code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable RMI instrumentation component and root cause as the advisory: ContextPayload.read(ObjectInput) deserializes attacker-controlled RMI context data with ObjectInput.readObject() before the 2.26.1 patch.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Debug mode logs all configuration values including secrets to stderr
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different component and root cause: debug configuration logging in AgentInitializer, not unsafe RMI context deserialization.

selected_finding.title: Unsafe deserialization in RMI context propagation allows arbitrary code execution

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory root cause in the same OpenTelemetry RMI instrumentation code. The retained runtime probe loaded the vulnerable v2.26.0 agent classdata and invoked ContextPayload.read(ObjectInput) with an attacker-controlled serialized Map subclass; the log and marker show the malicious readObject callback executed through that method. This proves activation of the advisory vulnerable logic, but it is a direct method harness rather than an end-to-end RMI/JMX network exploit.
evidence: /Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_6cf6a998/rmi_deserialization_rce/vulnerability_report.json identifies instrumentation/rmi/javaagent/src/main/java/io/opentelemetry/javaagent/instrumentation/rmi/context/ContextPayload.java:47 and ObjectInputStream.readObject() on RMI context data.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No retained runtime artifact shows the serialized payload delivered over a network-reachable RMI or JMX endpoint into an instrumented server JVM; the executed proof is a direct vulnerable-method activation harness.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The adapted probe loaded the vulnerable v2.26.0 OpenTelemetry agent ContextPayload classdata, verified the read(ObjectInput) bytecode contains ObjectInput.readObject, and invoked it with an attacker-controlled serialized Map subclass. The run log shows the malicious readObject callback executed and the marker file was created. This satisfies bug activation but not end-to-end RMI/JMX exploit success.
